title: strychnos axillaris
intro: Strychnos axillaris, also known as the Natal strychnine tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of southern Africa. The plant contains a toxic compound called strychnine, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the central nervous system and relieve pain. The health benefits of Strychnos axillaris include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, although its toxicity requires careful preparation and dosing. In herbal preparations, Strychnos axillaris is often used in small amounts, typically in combination with other herbs, to create tinctures and infusions that can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
